extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won from 7 places back in final 250m, overhauling Viviani's perfect Morkov-Richeze leadout train in extreme heat.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-Quickstep avoided front work until final 6km despite having yellow jersey, conserving Morkov for Viviani leadout.
Five-man break maintained 12-second gap at 4km despite four sprint teams chasing, suggesting coordinated energy conservation in 38°C.
Ewan launching from 7th wheel inside final 250m reveals superior positioning advantage over traditional long leadout trains.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: third crash in two weeks while defending GC position raises durability concerns for upcoming Alps
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: second win while penetrating furthest into any Grand Tour suggests his endurance ceiling was previously underestimated
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: negative: abandoned after crash at 27km while 9th on GC, eliminating pre-race favorite from contention
rider_signals.BAK Lars: neutral: 39-year-old prioritized KOM/intermediate points over break survival in final Tour, indicating farewell tour tactics
